title: Symptoms of Material Modes | BG 14.10 - 14.14 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Symptoms of material mode. What are the symptoms of person who is in Rajoguna, Tamoguna and Satyoguna?
Sometimes the mode of passion becomes prominent, defeating the mode of goodness, O son of Bharata.
And sometimes the mode of goodness defeats passion.
And at other times the mode of ignorance defeats goodness and passion.
Combination of all these modes are influencing us.
In this way there is always competition for supremacy.
When the mode of passion is prominent, the modes of goodness and ignorance is defeated.
Same is the case with ignorance.
When the ignorance is prominent, the influence of ignorance is prominent,
the goodness, the sense of goodness a person has and passion gets defeated.
It overpowers.
One can develop.
This is what progressive life is all about.
One can gradually with practice, by following the principles, following the recommended process,
one can come out of the influence of lower modes.
The prominence of some certain mode of nature is manifested in one's dealings, in his activities, in his eating.
Many, many years back I had gone for some work to Hong Kong.
And you know when you travel, you are eating, thinking about food.
When you travel, your eating thing gets affected to some extent.
And when I came back, I could really sense, because there where I was staying,
you know, you have to manage with some makeshift arrangements because you don't have...
So generally what we do is when we travel, we, you know, pack something or try if we can manage with some fruits.
Again, depends on how many days we travel.
You cannot be on fruits for 10 days at a stretch.
So I remember, you know, and it gets affected.
You get affected by the environment, the situation, people whom you are dabbling with, all that.
And because it was a long stay, it's difficult to manage.
So, you know, somebody had packed some pickles.
And when I came back, I could sense that the desire,
that inclination to have pickles increased drastically.
You will also notice, it's a very fine thing,
you will also notice, depending on what modes influencing us, your taste varies.
If it is sattva guna, you will like to eat a particular kind of foodstuffs.
If it is raja guna, another kind of masaledar, very fried, very this thing,
chatka, this, you know, chatpata.
And if it is in tamo guna,
I think I have shared this, from Hong Kong, we went to China.
So there was one place, that hotel, the USP of that hotel is everything is cooked fresh.
And in China, you know what is cooked.
So outside that hotel, there is,
you know, all kinds of reptiles, crabs, fishes, prawns, snakes, worms,
all live, fresh.
And morning, you know, we just stepped out of our place where we were staying,
so that hotel was there, and you know, three of us,
at a distance, we are wondering, what are these people eating?
I heard a saying that, in China,
whatever moves on the ground, except for, you know, cabs and trains, they eat.
Whatever moves in water, except submarines and ships, they eat.
Whatever flies in the sky, except for airplanes, they eat.
So, you know, the person, he took out a snake, live snake.
Somebody came in, the customer came in.
So the USP is that, you know, you can, as you enter the hotel, this thing,
you can order what you want to eat.
So you want to eat this crab, or you want to eat this prawns.
So they will take out live, it's cooked fresh, and served to you.
So we were, from a distance, watching all this,
and they took a snake, somebody had ordered that snake,
and the person, maybe, covered the face with some kind of a bag,
and dum, dum, two times he did a, you know, that, this thing.
The snake got caught, and then chopped.
Maybe that's the way of, of killing, I don't know.
So we were watching, and you know, completely dazed, what's happening,
and what people are eating.
Sometimes, you know, when you kill, when you cook some vegetables,
you get some worms, green worms.
There was one tray full of those green worms.
And all these worms are moving.
And it seems quite a, it seems quite a, it seems quite a, it seems quite a,
it seems quite a, it seems quite a, it seems quite a, it seems quite a, it seems quite a,
it seems quite a, it's a delicacy there.
Quite a cost leader.
So we were, we were watching, and from a distance,
what all these people are doing, what all these people are eating.
And this person who is, you know, dressing up the whole thing,
who is preparing for cooking, so he is also watching us.
And it went on for five, ten minutes.
And this person is not understanding as to why we are not entering the hotel
why we are not entering the hotel and ordering something?
Why are we just contemplating on what to eat
and standing there and not able to take a decision?
And after five minutes, he says,
he basically was asking us,
which one you want?
That's the taste.
That's the taste.
What is so obnoxious for us,
which we cannot even think of,
is a delicacy for them.
This reminds me of a story.
Prabhupada, when he went to Burma,
in Burma, there is a delicacy called nafi.
You can check on the net.
It's a very nice drink.
I'll tell you how it is prepared.
It's called nafi.
So what happens is,
when a person brings non-veg to their home,
so not everything can be cooked,
not everything is eaten.
There are some parts of the,
you know,
which cannot be cooked,
intestines or maybe, I don't know.
So what they do is,
instead of throwing away,
those parts are taken,
those parts are taken and dropped in a jar,
earthen jar, pitcher.
And the next day cooking happens.
Every day they cook non-veg.
So every day something gets deposited in that jar.
And it keeps accumulating.
And you know, meat doesn't stay, it decomposes.
So they keep adding, keep adding, keep adding, keep adding,
and it starts decaying, decomposing, decaying.
And you know, when it decays, all that liquid oozes out.
After three or four months,
they break open this jar, take out all that stuff,
and strain that liquid.
And that liquid is called nafi.
And especially when the festivals happen,
you invite the guests and all that.
Just like, you know, you have this,
you know, after all this, wine also depends on
how much time you have, how old it is.
So, six months old nafi.
One year old nafi.
And little by little, sip by sip,
it is served and they take it.
So that's the taste.
So, one day, the temple in Burma,
the devotees were there, Prabhupada,
so they were cooking a feast,
and they used, on that particular day,
desi ghee, pure ghee.
And when they were cooking puris in ghee,
all these neighbours, you know,
what are you doing?
What is this bad smell coming?
They started complaining.
For them, desi ghee is intolerable.
Bad smell.
Again, the influence of moths.
What is obnoxious for one person,
is nectar for another person.
What is nectar for one person,
is obnoxious for another person.
What is night for a materialist,
is a day for a spiritualist.
What is day for a spiritualist,
is a night for a materialist.
And why so much variety?
So much variety.
Somebody is eating snakes.
Eating dogs.
Influence of moths.
That's the taste.
That soul is under the spell of that moth.
That soul is under the spell of that moth.
It's eating that raw, live octopus.
It's eating that raw, live octopus.
You see on the sometimes,
it's very obnoxious, very difficult to see.
You see on the sometimes, it's very obnoxious, very difficult to see.
Live octopus.
You know, it's served.
They slit that thing and you actually,
with that spoon or fork,
you actually scoop out
and eat live.
And this is all not story,
it's happening.
Octopus, live.
Just slit that back portion,
it's very delicate
and then you scoop out
all that flesh part
and eat it.
There are people with that taste.
Therefore, one who is actually intent
in advancing in Krishna consciousness
should strive,
should strive
to come out of the influence
of these lower modes
and transcend the modes.
Treyagunyavishaya,
nishtreyagunyabhavarjuna.
Krishna says this.
All this thing is a game of three modes.
Treyagunyavishaya,
nishtreyagunyabhavarjuna.
You rise above the modes of material nature.
By transcending the modes of goodness,
one can be situated in pure goodness,
shuddha sattva.
So you have tamo guna,
you have rajo guna,
you have sattva guna.
Sattva guna is a springboard
for a person to jump to
shuddha sattva,
pure goodness
and that is
vishuddha sattva.
The manifestation of the modes of goodness
can be experienced
when all gates of the body
are illuminated with knowledge.
There are nine gates in the body.
Nine gates.
Anyone can help me with
what are the gates?
What are the nine?
Dwara.
Two eyes,
two ears,
two nostrils,
six.
Mouth,
anus and genitals.
Nine gates of this body.
When all the gates are illuminated with knowledge,
these gates dwell on the right things.
In every gate,
when the symptom of goodness is illuminated,
one sees, hears,
tastes the right thing.
Depending on the influence of modes,
our taste varies.
When I was staying outside
before joining the temple,
I think shared last session,
I was staying with my relatives
and
my cousin,
sister,
she somehow because of her company in school,
she was studying in a bishop carton school
and because of the company
and whatever the kind of friend circle,
she started eating non-veg.
Started eating non-veg.
Started liking non-veg.
And she would pester the mother,
you have to bring it at home too and all that.
And I could see that
what she likes eating
and we were together,
I mean we were staying,
I was staying with them
and spending lot of time with them and all that.
I could see that
more and more
because I was into Krishna Kanya,
more and more we are digressing
as far as our tastes are concerned.
What she wants me to eat,
I don't like.
What I like eating,
she doesn't like.
When I would be coming back home,
I would say,
oh tender coconuts,
we will have tender coconuts.
No brother,
I don't like it.
Tender coconut is not tasteful.
So this is the influence of mood.
It influences our eating,
the way we think,
thinking, feeling,
willing it influences.
One becomes cleansed inside and outside
when one transcends the mood.
The gate is illuminated with knowledge.
In every gate,
there is development of
symptom of happiness
and that is the position of goodness.
O chief of Bharatas,
when there is an increase
in the mode of passion,
the symptom of great attachment,
uncontrollable desire,
hankering
and intense endeavour develops.
When there is an increase
of influence of passion,
what will happen?
The symptoms are
great attachment,
uncontrollable desire.
Until I get it,
I cannot be happy.
I cannot just be happy.
I want this,
come what may,
hook or crook.
I want it.
I will do anything to get that.
There are people with that kind of a desire.
And for some petty gains,
okay,
could be some genuine thing makes sense,
but for some petty gains,
come what may,
I want this.
That's how you have sometimes
even brothers,
court cases,
things falling apart in the family.
Uncontrollable desire,
hankering
and intense endeavour develops.
One in the mode of passion
is never satisfied.
This is a symptom.
It's never satisfied.
Hope,
chase,
intense endeavour,
great attachment,
great attachment,
great attachment,
great attachment,
great attachment,
and one will endeavour to get indulge
and fulfilment is missing.
Always hoping,
chasing,
capturing,
indulging,
life is all about that.
So if you have intense desires
and one gets into all kinds of nefarious habits,
if he wants to construct a residential house,
he wants to build a house.
And what does he want?
to build a palatial house as if he is going to stay there for eternity.
A person in knowledge in Satoguna knows that yes house is required, a decent house is required,
a comfortable house is required and whatever is required he will build.
As he develops a great anchoring for sense satisfaction and increase, increasing sense
gratification becomes the goal.
A bigger house, a better house, a better desire, a better way of fulfilling the senses becomes
the goal.
That's the symptom of Rajoguna.
While we were talking this, lot of people say, you are saying that Rajoguna is intense
desire and then attraction between man and woman is symptom of this.
But Krishna also had many marriages.
When I was new to Krishna, this was the biggest problem which I faced when I was trying to
convince others about Krishna Kanjana.
All that is fine, but your Krishna, you say that all this is there, you have to control
the senses.
Your Krishna also had 1600 marriages, 16000 wives.
What is the answer for that?
Then some people say, people say that he should walk the talk.
There are many who say, control it, control it.
But then you know.
Why did he marry so many?
So the answer is, some of you may be aware, some of you may not be aware.
There was a demon by name Bhau Masura.
He actually captured 16,108 princes and imprisoned them, kept them with him in a fort.
And these princes, they were quite great devotees of the Lord and they prayed to the Lord, please
come and save us, please take us or rescue us from this captivity.
And Lord who is all powerful, he did actually go and kill the demon Bhau Masura and rescued
them.
In Vedic times, unlike present times, the girls, because it was very, chastity was a
very important thing.
These days, you know, it's relatively a very free society now.
Especially now, it's 12 o'clock, 1 o'clock.
You see, 12 o'clock, 1 o'clock, we see everyone moving around.
So in earlier times, a girl who has stayed out of the house captive with someone else,
nobody would marry.
It was unchaste.
We're not going into merits now.
We're not going to discuss into why it was considered and things like that.
So then, when Krishna rescued them, he said, now you go back home.
Be with your parents.
He says, no, now nobody will accept us.
We can't get married.
Even our parents won't accept us.
Because two days they were captive with Bhau Masura.
So then Krishna says, what do you want?
He says, we want you as our husband.
We want you to marry us.
So now Krishna, you know, sometimes this, he being the Lord, for him it's not a big
thing to.
Because you know, one wife, one has to think 10 times.
So for him, it's not a difficult thing.
So, okay, they were intensely desiring, praying for him to marry her, marry them.
He married.
And for each one of them, he made a separate palace.
It's not that each one has to wait for 16,107 nights to, for him to visit and be with them.
He made 16,108 palaces and expanded himself into 16,108 palaces.
And he was there personally present in each palace with each wife.
That is the omnipotency of the Lord.
So it is not, that's why it is said we have to understand all this from the right sources.
It is not that out of lust, out of the great desire that I will enjoy 16,108 wives, therefore
I marry so many times.
His devotees who were ardently praying for him to rescue and have him as their husband.
Okay, please come.
He being omnipotent, he can do that.
O son of Kunti, when there is an increase in the mode of ignorance, this is a symptom.
Madness, illusion, inertia, darkness are manifested.
So in Rajoguna, we saw the symptoms are great attachment, uncontrollable desire, hankering and intense endeavor.
Very passionate, always wanting, wanting, wanting, working hard, working hard, working hard with no knowledge.
And what is the symptom of Tamoguna?
When there is increase in mode of ignorance, madness, illusion, inertia.
Inertia means indolence, no desire for doing anything.
It could equate with so called depression people talk about.
No desire to do anything.
And darkness is manifested.
One wants to act whimsically and for no purpose.
And many times people say ignorance is bliss.
Ignorance is not bliss, it's dangerous.
It's bliss, maybe for a moment.
But I sometimes give this example, a person who is completely drunk.
For him to cross the river.
To cross the road and not meet with an accident, there is a possibility.
He is completely drunk and trying to cross.
Quite possible all the vehicles moving, the drivers may be more alert and they may stop and make him leave.
But the chances of probability of he meeting with an accident is much higher.
So a person who is in ignorance, for him to mess up his life is much higher.
Relatively speaking to a person in passion.
Even though he has capacity to work, he makes no endeavour.
He likes sleeping.
He doesn't want to take any initiative.
When one dies in the mode of goodness, he attained to pure higher planets.
In the material world also.
There are higher planets, there are lower planets.
And there are, just like even in this planet earth, we have countries which are much more, so to say, prosperous.
And the countries who are much more degraded or in the sense, less advanced.
So likewise, when a person who dies in the mode of goodness, dominantly in goodness, his destination is something else.
He goes to the planets where there is nothing.
He goes to the planets where predominantly sattvic people stay.
One in goodness attain higher planetary system like Brahmaloka or Janaloka and there enjoy godly happiness.
Those who die in mode of goodness are elevated to planets where great sages and great devotees live.
So we'll stop here.
Anyone has any question?
Anything based on, yes.
So one point, quite possible that I'm not aware of this story.
One thing is, about the Lord, not everything what we speak will satisfy people.
Many times it is not our arguments.
It is not our logic.
It is not what we are speaking.
It also depends on where that person is situated.
A person who is not in proper consciousness will always be blasphemous.
There are people like that.
Again, due respect to what you said.
We can try our best to present it in the best possible way.
But there is no one explanation which will completely satisfy them.
Again, the influence of modes.
A person who is deep in tamoguna, he will rebel.
Now you see, with respect to, let's say, eating non-veg.
Again, a person's taste, he is such lower consciousness.
You may even give logic.
You may even give best explanation.
You give scientific explanation, medical explanation.
A person cannot.
May not be problem with the explanation.
May not be problem with the logic.
Problem is, what consciousness the person is in.

But there is no one right explanation which will completely satisfy them.
So degradation, what the Lord, when he says that, the question is,
it's a good question, the question is,
even at times we see of great devotees, towards the end, last stages,
they are going through some circumstances,
very unfavorable circumstances.
So why did the Lord sanction that?
Srila Prabhupada, towards his last days in 1977,
he was in Vrindavan.
And, you know, his health was deteriorating day by day.
Physical health.
He was, he in fact, all his organs were started, you know, one by one, failing.
And some treatment was going on, but then,
it was very obvious that his health is deteriorating.
Organs were failing.
And, he was on the bed, lying.
But till the last date, we have videos on that.
There's a video called, The Final Lesson.
Maybe we can check on the net, if it's available.
Otherwise, we'll show some time here.
Till the last date.
The body is in pain.
You know, organs are failing.
You're not able to eat and all those things.
But you will see, his consciousness is crystal clear.
Till the last date.
Till the last day of his departure,
he is dictating the Bhaktivedanta purpose.
So, what the Lord protects, in case of devotees,
is not, because the body, today or tomorrow, it has to deteriorate and,
it's not that a person is a devotee, he will not become old,
he will not become diseased.
He will.
The nature's law will act.
But what the Lord protects,
is the consciousness.
Because it is not that we should also not have false expectation that,
now I am a devotee, so, diseases won't come.
As per the law of nature, it will come.
But what is real protection,
sometimes we also worry, you know,
I am going to the temple, I am doing this, I am also chanting,
but you know, my health has started deteriorating.
Why it is like that?
Because physical health, beyond a point,
okay, we can just delay the whole process.
A person has to die at the age of 80,
maybe with all good care of the health,
you can add this stretch to 100 years,
or 110 years, maybe.
But anyway, the decay has to happen.
Any material thing goes through 6 transits,
decay and finally diseases,
is part of the whole process.
What the Lord protects is,
not in illusion, He protects the body.
Because our idea of protection is the body,
which anyway cannot be protected.
What the Lord is really interested in protecting,
and which He promises He will protect,
for the devotees, is His consciousness.
That consciousness which has,
which can go into the spell of,
what we talked about, Tamoguna,
indolence, madness, illusion,
He protects from that,
and that is real protection.
So we have great devotees,
like I gave example of Prabhupada,
last day, all organs are failing,
body is in extreme pain,
he was called medically,
but his consciousness is pristine,
clear,
clear Krishna conscious.
You should see that video to believe it.
Everything is moving,
because of the presence of,
presence of Lord Shri Krishna.
This consciousness is called Krishna conscious.
Yes.
